title: amaryllis belladonna subsp. barbata
intro: Amaryllis belladonna subsp. barbata, also known as the Naked Lady or Belladonna, is a perennial bulbous plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its striking, funnel-shaped flowers with six tepals. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used in herbal medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Amaryllis belladonna subsp. barbata include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution should be exercised due to its toxicity and potential side effects when ingested.
